summary: Large Yoshi special-move decomp PR focused on ftYs_SpecialS, with a smaller ftYs_SpecialHi egg-throw callback improvement. It replaced many `/// #` stubs with C, expanded Yoshi attribute and motion-var layouts, typed item prototypes, and produced 9 new full matches plus 11 near/full improvements according to decomp.dev.

key_files.role: Main implementation work for Yoshi SpecialS handlers, helpers, physics, collision, animation, effects, and callback setup.
Implemented `fn_8012E110` egg item accessory callback and adjusted matching helpers/includes.
Expanded `ftYoshiAttributes`, `ftYs_DatAttrs`, and `ftYoshi_MotionVars.specials` fields used by the new decomp.
Replaced unknown item function prototypes with concrete `it_802B28C8` and `it_802B2A10` signatures.
Kept cross-header prototype for `ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48` in sync.

what_changed: Implemented many Yoshi `ftYs_SpecialS` functions that were previously `/// #` placeholders, including `ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48`, `ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC`, `ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_IASA`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Phys`, and `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Phys`.

Added near-matching C for several larger SpecialS animation/collision handlers, including `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Anim`, and `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0/1/2/3_Coll`.
Added SpecialS helper routines for callback setup, wall-bounce effect spawning, shell/body visibility stepping, scale updates, rotation wrapping, and button-pressure checking.
Changed `ftYs_Unk3_803CEDA4` from a `Vec3` array shape to a single `ftCollisionBox`, enabling calls such as `ft_80082888`, `ft_80082978`, and `ft_800824A0` to use the right helper type.

Implemented `fn_8012E110` in `ftYs_SpecialHi.c`: it checks `throw_flags_b0`, spawns a Yoshi egg item with `it_802B2A10`, sets damage/death callbacks while the egg is held, throws it through `it_802B28C8`, then clears the held item pointer and callbacks.

Expanded Yoshi data layouts by replacing padding with fields in `ftYoshiAttributes` from roughly `x54` through `xE8`, `ftYs_DatAttrs` fields `x104`, `x108`, `x10C`, `x110`, and SpecialS motion vars `x4`, `x8`, `x28`, `x2C`.
Replaced `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes for `ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48`, `it_802B28C8`, and `it_802B2A10` with concrete signatures.
decomp.dev reported matched code at 58.47%, a +0.14% improvement and +5484 bytes, with 9 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched items.
smart_moves: Typing `ftYs_Unk3_803CEDA4` as `ftCollisionBox` was a high-leverage correction because multiple collision functions shared that object.
The PR unlocked many functions by first exposing the needed Yoshi attribute and motion-var offsets instead of continuing to work around padding.

Small `static inline` helpers such as `ftYs_SpecialS_UpdateScale`, `ftYs_SpecialS_WrapAndSetRotX`, and `ftYs_SpecialS_SpawnWallBounceEffect` captured repeated compiler-shaped logic without over-naming every anonymous state field.
The `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C_outline` wrapper and repeated small scopes/local temporaries show deliberate codegen control for matching rather than only source readability.
Item header typing for `it_802B28C8` and `it_802B2A10` made the SpecialHi egg item flow expressible with `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `s32`, and `f32` instead of opaque unknown params.
The implementation preserved callback lifecycle details: setting `death2_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`, `deal_dmg_cb`, and `x21F8` across transitions, and clearing held-egg damage callbacks after the egg throw.
decomp_lessons: For fighter move decomp, struct/header work can be the blocker: replacing padding with offset-accurate attribute fields and motion-vars can unlock many functions at once.
Collision helper signatures often reveal the true static-data type; a pair of `Vec3` values may actually be an `ftCollisionBox` when passed to fighter collision routines.

Callbacks are part of the behavior, not cleanup noise. Matching action-state code must preserve when `take_dmg_cb`, `death2_cb`, `deal_dmg_cb`, accessory callbacks, and `x21F8` are installed or cleared.
Wall-bounce code combines collision env flags, wall normals, ECB offsets, effect spawn IDs, camera calls, and velocity rewrites; future collision decomp should keep these concerns together.
Part indices matter for matching and semantics: this PR uses joints such as `fp->parts[4].joint` and `fp->parts[31].joint` for effect/item positions rather than generic `fp->parts->joint` access.
Near-match work is still valuable when it gives typed fields and shared helpers; the bot showed several collision/animation functions at about 98-99% even when not fully matched.
Manual-looking ABS/sign handling, duplicate `GET_FIGHTER` locals, and nested scopes may be intentional matching tactics and should not be simplified casually.
No PR body or human review comments were present in the slice, so reviewer intent beyond the merge and bot metrics is not evidenced.
naming_conventions: Unknown exported functions retained address-style names such as `ftYs_SpecialS_8012EB48`, `ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC`, `ftYs_SpecialS_8012F35C`, and `fn_8012E110`.

New private helpers used descriptive `ftYs_SpecialS_` names, for example `ftYs_SpecialS_UpdateScale`, `ftYs_SpecialS_WrapAndSetRotX`, `ftYs_SpecialS_CheckButtonPressure`, and `ftYs_SpecialS_SpawnWallBounceEffect`.
Yoshi attribute fields remained offset-style (`x54`, `x58`, `xA4`, `xD8`, `xE8`) where semantics were not proven.
SpecialS motion vars remained offset-style (`x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`, `x18`, `x24`, `x28`, `x2C`, `x30`) but gained concrete scalar types and fp offset comments.
Header prototypes kept address comments while replacing unknown signatures with concrete `Fighter_GObj*`, `Item_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `s32`, and `f32` parameters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` with several sizes, including 4, 8, 16, 28, and 40 bytes, to preserve stack layout.
Kept nested scopes and repeated `Fighter* fpN = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` locals in places where a simpler source form would likely change register allocation.
Used `static inline` helpers and an explicit outline wrapper for `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C` to steer inlining and call shape.
Indexed `Vec4` scale tables through `(f32*) &ftYs_Unk3_803CED84` and `(f32*) &ftYs_Unk3_803CED94`, matching data-layout/codegen expectations.
Used `M_PI`, `M_PI_2`, `atan2f`, and explicit f64-to-f32 style expressions for rotation math.
Kept magic motion IDs and flags such as `0x165`, `0x167`, `0x169`, `0x16A`, `0x16B`, `0x440012`, `0x4C4092`, `0x4C4892`, and `0x0C4C509A` where no enum naming evidence was provided.
Kept wall env-flag masks `0x3F` and `0xFC0` to distinguish facing-right and facing-left wall checks.
Used `mpLib_800569EC(fp->coll_data.floor.flags)` as a multiplier in ground physics acceleration updates.
Swapped a custom inline `max` helper for the project `MAX` macro in `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF8C`.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code report for GALE01 showed 58.47% matched code, +0.14%, +5484 bytes.
review_feedback.evidence: Bot comment listed 9 new matches and 11 improvements.
Issue comment table under `✅ 9 new matches`.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments, review records, or PR body were present in the provided slice.
review_feedback.evidence: Counts show 0 review comments and 0 reviews; PR body is null.

follow_up_queries: Finish or inspect remaining near-matches reported by decomp.dev: `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_2_Coll`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_3_Coll`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLanding_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_Coll`, `ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_Coll`, and `fn_8012E110`.
Name `ftYoshiAttributes` fields `x54` through `xE8` once SpecialS behavior semantics are confirmed.
Name SpecialS motion vars such as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`, `x18`, `x24`, `x28`, `x2C`, and `x30` after mapping their timer, scale-index, rotation, turn, bounce, and speed roles.
Replace raw motion IDs and flags in this file with enums or named constants if neighboring Yoshi action states establish names.
Confirm the semantic names and parameter meanings for `it_802B28C8` and `it_802B2A10` from item-side decomp.
Investigate whether `ftYs_Unk3_803CED48`, `ftYs_Unk3_803CED84`, and `ftYs_Unk3_803CED94` can be given descriptive names tied to visibility/scale animation.
